articles.title: Gov. Cox stands for Utah's anti-gambling policy amidst legal battle with Kalshi
articles.summary: Utah Governor Spencer Cox is defending the state's strict anti-gambling stance while facing a legal challenge from prediction market Kalshi. The fight matters because Utah remains one of the few states with zero tolerance for any form of wagering, making it a key battleground for whether prediction markets can operate nationwide without carve-outs.

articles.title: More Evidence Emerges of Iran War Insider Trading
articles.summary: New reports suggest traders with advance knowledge of Iran's military actions have been profiting from strategic bets on defense stocks, oil futures, and geopolitical markets. If proven, this pattern could signal that prediction markets are being systematically gamed by those with access to classified intelligence, making crowd wisdom less reliable when it matters most.

articles.title: FIS Launches New Cleared Derivatives Solution for Prediction Markets
articles.summary: FIS, the massive financial technology company, just rolled out a new clearing solution specifically designed for prediction markets derivatives. This could legitimize prediction markets in the eyes of traditional finance by bringing institutional-grade infrastructure to what's still largely a retail playground.

articles.title: Exclusive: Trader made nearly $1 million on Polymarket with remarkably accurate Iran bets
articles.summary: A trader scored nearly $1 million on Polymarket by nailing a series of Iran-related bets with uncanny precision. This shows how geopolitical expertise can translate into massive prediction market profits, likely attracting more serious money and talent to these platforms.

articles.title: Analysis | Taking a gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: Another mainstream outlet is examining whether prediction markets deserve serious attention as forecasting tools. The coverage signals growing institutional curiosity about betting markets, which typically precedes either regulatory crackdowns or widespread adoption.

articles.title: Lawmakers Introduce Bipartisan Bill Banning Sports Bets on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Bipartisan lawmakers want to ban sports betting on prediction markets, targeting platforms that let users wager on game outcomes alongside political and economic events. The move threatens to fragment the prediction market ecosystem by forcing platforms to choose between sports content that drives user engagement and the regulatory legitimacy they need to operate.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.

articles.title: Opinion | We Shouldn’t Want a World Run by Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets shouldn't become the dominant force in decision-making across society. This pushback matters because it signals growing concern about market-based governance as prediction platforms gain mainstream credibility and political influence.
